pages.raw_text:
The sign boards from the Carr
Adams & Switzer and Washington
employment bureaus were smashed
and cast into the river off Stevens
street bridge last night.


The work is evidently that of
stragglers from the gang of rioters
that demolished the furniture in
the Red Cross employment agency
between Front and Main avenues
on Stevens street week ago.


The boards torn from the front
of the Carr employment bureau,
corner Front avenue and Stevens
street, was secured to the brick
wall with iron clamps with long
shanks driven several inches into
the masonry. That several men
figured in the disorder is evident
from the fact that nearly all the
irons that were not removed and
thrown away with the boards were
bent out of shape and left in the
wall.


AS result of the trouble, the
men have armed them
selves and closer surveillance is
to be kept by the police in the
Office districts.


Possibly these conditions will be
permitted until somebody is badly
hurt or killed Complaints of en-
ployment agency swindles have
come in constantly for the past
two years without awaking any
thing in the nature of systematic
relief from the authorities, and
now the opposite extreme has been
reached apparently.
